pr.title: [WIP] Transform portfolio into interactive Linux desktop experience
pr.body:
Thanks for asking me to work on this. I will get started on it and keep this PR's description up to date as I form a plan and make progress.


<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

Transform the portfolio into a fully interactive, nostalgia-driven Linux desktop experience, directly on the main branch. Key requirements:

- Replace the homepage with a Linux-style boot sequence and login screen. Login requires no password, just a button click to enter.
- Desktop environment with a classic Linux wallpaper/background (choose a default like GNOME, KDE, or Ubuntu), taskbar/panel (with clock & app launcher), and optional desktop icons for main apps.
- Apps run in draggable, resizable windows using a window manager approach. Each window must have close, minimize (and optionally, maximize) functionality.
- Include realistic, minimal versions of these apps: Calculator, Terminal (funny/mock commands like 'ls', 'about', 'help'), File Manager (show Home, Documents, etc.), Text Editor (basic), About Me/Projects (as a system app linked to your real content).
- Remove prior portfolio-type effects, animations, or unnecessary visuals—strictly realistic Linux UX only.
- Include authentic boot/login sounds and optionally window open/close sounds.
- Keep code organized: separate desktop, window manager, and apps into sensible React (or component-based TypeScript) structure. Use CSS for styling, matching classic Linux UI elements and fonts.
- Ensure the whole experience works smoothly, responsively, and without errors. Polish details: right-click menus, app/taskbar icons, classic cursors, font choices, etc. 

Goal: A super-realistic, interactive Linux desktop website portfolio indistinguishable from the nostalgia experience of omarchy.com.

The following is the prior conversation context from the user's chat exploration (may be truncated):

User: hello

Assistant: Hello! How can I help you with the nrnnaveen/naveen--portfolio repository today?

User: like in my portfolio you want me to optimize it into real linux style portfolio like the booting system login sytem add linux wallpaper minimimal looks like real all things on it dont over do like glowing matrix and live things make it like an real linux with super real linux sound effects add some common basic app on it all are want to working fine make it super ultra realistic linux style portfolio without any errors make it and remove the extra unnessacery effects and make it supercool nostaligia linux

Assistant: That's an awesome idea! You're looking to transform your portfolio (nrnnaveen/naveen--portfolio) into a super realistic, minimal Linux-style experience with the following features:

**Requirements:**

- Super ultra-realistic Linux style UI (not flashy or animated like Matrix, but classic/nostalgic and minimal)
- Booting animation similar to a real Linux system
- Login screen mimicking Linux login (username/password prompt—even if only for effect)
- Desktop with Linux wallpaper (classic/nostalgic wallpapers, e.g., GNOME, KDE, or Xfce)
- Minimal UI—no extra effects, just "real Linux" look and feel
- Authentic Linux sound effects (boot, login, notification sounds)
- Basic apps ("working," i.e., interactive): e.g., terminal, file manager, text editor, maybe browser/about window, calculator, system settings, etc.
- Remove extra/unnecessary effects and previous portfolio-only elements
- Everything should feel real and polished—no obvious fake or unfinished features

**Before we start:**
A couple of quick questions to tailor the implementation:

1. What tech stack/framework is your portfolio currently using? (React, plain HTML/CSS/JS, Vue, etc.)
2. Do you have a favorite Linux distro or desktop environment (e.g., Ubuntu/GNOME, KDE Plasma, Xfce, or even old-school like GNOME 2, MATE, or LXDE)?
3. Do you want the login to be functional (require a password, even if hardcoded), or just for visual effect?
4. Should the apps open in draggable/resizable windows (like a modern desktop), or fixed sections?
5. Are there any “basic apps” you especially want (please list preferences: e.g., terminal, about, projects, calculator), or can I make recommendations?

Once you reply, I can outline a high-level plan and start preparing the PR steps for your repo!

User: add style like nostalgia super like omarchy / like pas want but login works without password also, apps open in draggable/resizable windows (like a modern desktop,like calculator , some basic application really in omarchy like how omarchy actual like that i want make exactly like that 

Assistant: Great! Here’s a summary of exactly what you’re looking for:

- Theme: Nostalgic Linux—super similar to omarchy (https://omarchy.com/) or pas—authentic, classic, ultra-realistic Linux desktop.
- Login Screen: Should look authentic but should work without entering a password (just hit “login”).
- Desktop: Real Linux wallpaper/background, minimal/nostalgic look.
- Apps: Basic apps such as calculator, file manager, terminal, text editor, etc.
- Windows: Apps open in draggable and resizable windows just like on a real Linux desktop (as in omarchy).
- Tech Stack: Your portfolio repo uses TypeScript, CSS, and HTML.
- UX:...

</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

*This pull request was created from Copilot chat.*
>
pr.author: Copilot
